description: Per-language HTTP / URL-fetching sinks that, when reached with user-controlled input, constitute SSRF. Group by language family; pair each sink with the wrapped, allowlist-enforcing alternative.

sinks.safer: Wrap with a fetch_external(url) helper that calls socket.getaddrinfo, validates the resolved IP against a denylist (RFC1918, 169.254/16, ::/128, fc00::/7, IPv4-mapped IPv6), and connects to the pinned IP with `requests.get('https://' + pinned_ip, headers={'Host': hostname}, verify=hostname, allow_redirects=False)`.
Use undici with a custom `connect` that resolves once, checks against the deny list, and pins the IP. Set `redirect: 'manual'` and re-run the validator on each `location:` header.
Wrap in SsrfGuardedHttpClient that overrides ProxySelector / Resolver to enforce IP allowlist; set followRedirects(NEVER); reject schemes other than http/https.

Use a *http.Client with a custom DialContext that calls net.Resolver.LookupIPAddr, checks every IP against the deny list, then dials the first allowed one. Set CheckRedirect to re-run validation per hop and CheckRedirect = ErrUseLastResponse when redirects are not needed.
Use http.rb or Faraday with a connection adapter that does the resolve-then-pin pattern. Disable URI.open / Kernel#open for URLs.
Construct HttpClient with SocketsHttpHandler.ConnectCallback that resolves and IP-filters; set AllowAutoRedirect = false.
Disable allow_url_fopen / allow_url_include in php.ini. Use a wrapper that calls gethostbynamel + filter_var(FILTER_VALIDATE_IP, FILTER_FLAG_NO_PRIV_RANGE).

anti_patterns.rule: Differential URL parsers (e.g. WHATWG URL vs RFC 3986) routinely cause allowlist bypass; pick one parser and stick with it
Resolve once, validate, connect to pinned IP — otherwise the resolver can rebind between check and connect
Following redirects re-introduces SSRF on every hop; disable or re-validate on each hop
gopher://, dict://, file://, jar://, ldap://, netdoc:// are all SSRF amplifiers; restrict to http/https
169.254.169.254 / [fd00:ec2::254] / metadata.google.internal must be blocked at network and app layer
AWS EC2 must enforce IMDSv2 with hop-limit=1; IMDSv1 was the Capital One 2019 breach vector
Reflecting the user URL in error responses can turn blind SSRF into data exfiltration; never echo the URL
